revision.comment: 岐佐神社（浜松市）の記事を新規作成
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 岐佐神社 |画像= [[File:Kisa-jinja-haiden.jpg|thumb|center|]] |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[浜松市]]西区舞阪町舞阪1973 |位置 = |祭神 = [[蚶貝比賣命]]
[[蛤貝比賣命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 旧暦9月14日・15日 |神事 = }} '''岐佐神社'''（きさじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[浜松市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == * [[蚶貝比賣命]] - アカガイの女神 * [[蛤貝比賣命]] - ハマグリの女神 == 概要 == [[File:Kis-jinja.jpg|thumb|岐佐神社の鳥居]] 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』（927年）に、「[[遠江国]]六十二座、敷智郡六座の一座」との記載がある[[式内社]]。地域の氏神であると共に、水産・漁業の守り神として漁民の信仰を集めてきた{{Cite web |author = 岐佐神社 |url = http://www.kisa-jinja.jp/about |title = 岐佐神社の由緒 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 == 歴史 == [[明応]]の津波（1498年）により、舞澤の郷と共に流されたが、東の松林（現在地）に神祠が漂着したため、その地に社殿を造営して祀った。 [[天正]]2年（1574年）に本殿を再建。[[慶長]]6年（1601年）には伊奈備前守より墨印石高三石を与えられ、三代将軍[[徳川家光]]以下、歴代将軍より朱印五石を与えられた。 [[明治]]6年、[[郷社]]に列せられた。[[大正]]元年に本殿を造営、[[大正]]13年に神饌幣帛料供進社の指定を受けた{{Cite web |author = 静岡県神社庁 |url = http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4413057 |title = 岐佐神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 平成22年の修復事業により本殿の立て起こし、拝殿・覆屋の屋根瓦葺き替えなどを実施した。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:きさしんしや}} [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 岐佐神社 |画像= [[File:Kisa-jinja-haiden.jpg|thumb|center|]] |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[浜松市]]西区舞阪町舞阪1973 |位置 = |祭神 = [[蚶貝比賣命]]
[[蛤貝比賣命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 旧暦9月14日・15日 |神事 = }} '''岐佐神社'''（きさじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[浜松市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == * [[蚶貝比賣命]] - アカガイの女神 * [[蛤貝比賣命]] - ハマグリの女神 == 概要 == [[File:Kis-jinja.jpg|thumb|岐佐神社の鳥居]] 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』（927年）に、「[[遠江国]]六十二座、敷智郡六座の一座」との記載がある[[式内社]]。地域の氏神であると共に、水産・漁業の守り神として漁民の信仰を集めてきた{{Cite web |author = 岐佐神社 |url = http://www.kisa-jinja.jp/about |title = 岐佐神社の由緒 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 == 歴史 == [[明応]]の津波（1498年）により、舞澤の郷と共に流されたが、東の松林（現在地）に神祠が漂着したため、その地に社殿を造営して祀った。 [[天正]]2年（1574年）に本殿を再建。[[慶長]]6年（1601年）には伊奈備前守より墨印石高三石を与えられ、三代将軍[[徳川家光]]以下、歴代将軍より朱印五石を与えられた。 [[明治]]6年、[[郷社]]に列せられた。[[大正]]元年に本殿を造営、[[大正]]13年に神饌幣帛料供進社の指定を受けた{{Cite web |author = 静岡県神社庁 |url = http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4413057 |title = 岐佐神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 平成22年の修復事業により本殿の立て起こし、拝殿・覆屋の屋根瓦葺き替えなどを実施した。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:きさしんしや}} [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]]

]] 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』（927年）に、「[[遠江国]]六十二座、敷智郡六座の一座」との記載がある[[式内社]]。地域の氏神であると共に、水産・漁業の守り神として漁民の信仰を集めてきた{{Cite web |author = 岐佐神社 |url = http://www.kisa-jinja.jp/about |title = 岐佐神社の由緒 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 == 歴史 == [[明応]]の津波（1498年）により、舞澤の郷と共に流されたが、東の松林（現在地）に神祠が漂着したため、その地に社殿を造営して祀った。 [[天正]]2年（1574年）に本殿を再建。[[慶長]]6年（1601年）には伊奈備前守より墨印石高三石を与えられ、三代将軍[[徳川家光]]以下、歴代将軍より朱印五石を与えられた。 [[明治]]6年、[[郷社]]に列せられた。[[大正]]元年に本殿を造営、[[大正]]13年に神饌幣帛料供進社の指定を受けた{{Cite web |author = 静岡県神社庁 |url = http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4413057 |title = 岐佐神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 平成22年の修復事業により本殿の立て起こし、拝殿・覆屋の屋根瓦葺き替えなどを実施した。 == 摂末社 == 摂末社・境内関係社は次の通り。 '''境内社''' * ごしんさま（護神様・御親様）　：　天白社と、 伊勢神宮・津島神社・春日大社の分霊を祀る。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}

{{神社 |名称 = 岐佐神社 |画像= [[File:Kisa-jinja-haiden.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[浜松市]][[中央区 (浜松市)|中央区]]舞阪町舞阪1973 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|蚶貝比賣命]]
[[キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|蛤貝比賣命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 旧暦9月14日・15日 |神事 = }} '''岐佐神社'''（きさじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[浜松市]][[中央区 (浜松市)|中央区]]舞坂町に鎮座する[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == * [[キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|蚶貝比賣命]] - アカガイの女神 * [[キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|蛤貝比賣命]] - ハマグリの女神 == 概要 == [[File:Kis-jinja.jpg|thumb|
岐佐神社の鳥居
]] 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』（927年）に、「[[遠江国]]六十二座、敷智郡六座の一座」との記載がある[[式内社]]。地域の氏神であると共に、水産・漁業の守り神として漁民の信仰を集めてきた{{Cite web|和書 |author = 岐佐神社 |url = http://www.kisa-jinja.jp/about |title = 岐佐神社の由緒 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 == 歴史 == [[明応]]の津波（1498年）により、舞澤の郷と共に流されたが、東の松林（現在地）に神祠が漂着したため、その地に社殿を造営して祀った。 [[天正]]2年（1574年）に本殿を再建。[[慶長]]6年（1601年）には伊奈備前守より墨印石高三石を与えられ、三代将軍[[徳川家光]]以下、歴代将軍より朱印五石を与えられた。 [[明治]]6年、[[郷社]]に列せられた。[[大正]]元年に本殿を造営、[[大正]]13年に神饌幣帛料供進社の指定を受けた{{Cite web|和書 |author = 静岡県神社庁 |url = http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4413057 |title = 岐佐神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 平成22年の修復事業により本殿の立て起こし、拝殿・覆屋の屋根瓦葺き替えなどを実施した。 == 摂末社 == 摂末社・境内関係社は次の通り。 '''境内社''' * ごしんさま（護神様・御親様）　：　天白社と、 伊勢神宮・津島神社・春日大社の分霊を祀る。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:きさしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:浜松市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:浜松市中央区の建築物]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 岐佐神社 |画像= [[File:Kisa-jinja-haiden.jpg|250px]]

{{nihongo|'''Kisa Shrine'''|岐佐神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kisa Shrine'''|岐佐神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kisa Shrine'''|岐佐神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kisa Shrine'''|岐佐神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kisa Shrine'''|岐佐神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]].

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kisa Shrine | native_name = 岐佐神社 | image = Kisa-jinja-haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Kisa Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.683828|137.610955}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Kisagai-hime and Umugi-hime|lt=Kisagai-hime and Umugi-hime|ja|キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|zh|蚶貝比賣與蛤貝比賣|WD=Q11296639}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Kisa Shrine'''|岐佐神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]].

revision.comment: 18 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 岐佐神社 |画像= [[File:Kisa-jinja-haiden.jpg|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[浜松市]][[中央区 (浜松市)|中央区]][[舞阪町]]舞阪1973 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|蚶貝比賣命]]
[[キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|蛤貝比賣命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 旧暦9月14日・15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=岐佐神社|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''岐佐神社'''（きさじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[浜松市]][[中央区 (浜松市)|中央区]]舞阪町に鎮座する[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == * [[キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|蚶貝比賣命]] - アカガイの女神 * [[キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|蛤貝比賣命]] - ハマグリの女神 == 概要 == [[File:Kis-jinja.jpg|thumb|
岐佐神社の鳥居
]] 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』（927年）に、「[[遠江国]]六十二座、敷智郡六座の一座」との記載がある[[式内社]]。地域の氏神であると共に、水産・漁業の守り神として漁民の信仰を集めてきた{{Cite web|和書 |author = 岐佐神社 |url = http://www.kisa-jinja.jp/about |title = 岐佐神社の由緒 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 == 歴史 == [[明応]]の津波（1498年）により、舞澤の郷と共に流されたが、東の松林（現在地）に神祠が漂着したため、その地に社殿を造営して祀った。 [[天正]]2年（1574年）に本殿を再建。[[慶長]]6年（1601年）には伊奈備前守より墨印石高三石を与えられ、三代将軍[[徳川家光]]以下、歴代将軍より朱印五石を与えられた。 [[明治]]6年、[[郷社]]に列せられた。[[大正]]元年に本殿を造営、[[大正]]13年に神饌幣帛料供進社の指定を受けた{{Cite web|和書 |author = 静岡県神社庁 |url = http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4413057 |title = 岐佐神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 平成22年の修復事業により本殿の立て起こし、拝殿・覆屋の屋根瓦葺き替えなどを実施した。 == 摂末社 == 摂末社・境内関係社は次の通り。 '''境内社''' * ごしんさま（護神様・御親様）　：　天白社と、 伊勢神宮・津島神社・春日大社の分霊を祀る。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:きさしんしや}} [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:浜松市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:浜松市中央区の建築物]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[岐佐神社]] to [[Kisa Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Merging Japanese Wikipedia import with English content
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 岐佐神社 |画像= [[File:Kisa-jinja-haiden.jpg|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[浜松市]][[中央区 (浜松市)|中央区]][[舞阪町]]舞阪1973 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|蚶貝比賣命]]
[[キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|蛤貝比賣命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 旧暦9月14日・15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=岐佐神社|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''岐佐神社'''（きさじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[浜松市]][[中央区 (浜松市)|中央区]]舞阪町に鎮座する[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == * [[キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|蚶貝比賣命]] - アカガイの女神 * [[キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|蛤貝比賣命]] - ハマグリの女神 == 概要 == [[File:Kis-jinja.jpg|thumb|
岐佐神社の鳥居
]] 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』（927年）に、「[[遠江国]]六十二座、敷智郡六座の一座」との記載がある[[式内社]]。地域の氏神であると共に、水産・漁業の守り神として漁民の信仰を集めてきた{{Cite web|和書 |author = 岐佐神社 |url = http://www.kisa-jinja.jp/about |title = 岐佐神社の由緒 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 == 歴史 == [[明応]]の津波（1498年）により、舞澤の郷と共に流されたが、東の松林（現在地）に神祠が漂着したため、その地に社殿を造営して祀った。 [[天正]]2年（1574年）に本殿を再建。[[慶長]]6年（1601年）には伊奈備前守より墨印石高三石を与えられ、三代将軍[[徳川家光]]以下、歴代将軍より朱印五石を与えられた。 [[明治]]6年、[[郷社]]に列せられた。[[大正]]元年に本殿を造営、[[大正]]13年に神饌幣帛料供進社の指定を受けた{{Cite web|和書 |author = 静岡県神社庁 |url = http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4413057 |title = 岐佐神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 平成22年の修復事業により本殿の立て起こし、拝殿・覆屋の屋根瓦葺き替えなどを実施した。 == 摂末社 == 摂末社・境内関係社は次の通り。 '''境内社''' * ごしんさま（護神様・御親様）　：　天白社と、 伊勢神宮・津島神社・春日大社の分霊を祀る。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:きさしんしや}} [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:浜松市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:浜松市中央区の建築物]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]]

]] 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』（927年）に、「[[遠江国]]六十二座、敷智郡六座の一座」との記載がある[[式内社]]。地域の氏神であると共に、水産・漁業の守り神として漁民の信仰を集めてきた{{Cite web|和書 |author = 岐佐神社 |url = http://www.kisa-jinja.jp/about |title = 岐佐神社の由緒 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 == 歴史 == [[明応]]の津波（1498年）により、舞澤の郷と共に流されたが、東の松林（現在地）に神祠が漂着したため、その地に社殿を造営して祀った。 [[天正]]2年（1574年）に本殿を再建。[[慶長]]6年（1601年）には伊奈備前守より墨印石高三石を与えられ、三代将軍[[徳川家光]]以下、歴代将軍より朱印五石を与えられた。 [[明治]]6年、[[郷社]]に列せられた。[[大正]]元年に本殿を造営、[[大正]]13年に神饌幣帛料供進社の指定を受けた{{Cite web|和書 |author = 静岡県神社庁 |url = http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4413057 |title = 岐佐神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 平成22年の修復事業により本殿の立て起こし、拝殿・覆屋の屋根瓦葺き替えなどを実施した。 == 摂末社 == 摂末社・境内関係社は次の通り。 '''境内社''' * ごしんさま（護神様・御親様）　：　天白社と、 伊勢神宮・津島神社・春日大社の分霊を祀る。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:浜松市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:浜松市中央区の建築物]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] ==End Japanese==

{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:浜松市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:浜松市中央区の建築物]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]]

[[File:Kisa-jinja-haiden.jpg|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|浜松市|Q185125}}{{jalink|中央区 (浜松市)|Q117084743|中央区}}{{jalink|舞阪町|Q11613666}}舞阪1973 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|Q11296639|蚶貝比賣命}}
{{jalink|キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|Q11296639|蛤貝比賣命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 旧暦9月14日・15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=岐佐神社|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''岐佐神社'''（きさじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|浜松市|Q185125}}{{jalink|中央区 (浜松市)|Q117084743|中央区}}舞阪町に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|Q11296639|蚶貝比賣命}} - アカガイの女神 * {{jalink|キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|Q11296639|蛤貝比賣命}} - ハマグリの女神 === 概要 === [[File:Kis-jinja.jpg|thumb|
岐佐神社の鳥居
]] 『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』（927年）に、「{{jalink|遠江国|Q1048864}}六十二座、敷智郡六座の一座」との記載がある{{jalink|式内社}}。地域の氏神であると共に、水産・漁業の守り神として漁民の信仰を集めてきた{{Cite web|和書 |author = 岐佐神社 |url = https://www.kisa-jinja.jp/about |title = 岐佐神社の由緒 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|明応|Q1193841}}の津波（1498年）により、舞澤の郷と共に流されたが、東の松林（現在地）に神祠が漂着したため、その地に社殿を造営して祀った。 {{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}2年（1574年）に本殿を再建。{{jalink|慶長|Q1328185}}6年（1601年）には伊奈備前守より墨印石高三石を与えられ、三代将軍{{jalink|徳川家光|Q314481}}以下、歴代将軍より朱印五石を与えられた。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年、{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列せられた。{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}元年に本殿を造営、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}13年に神饌幣帛料供進社の指定を受けた{{Cite web|和書 |author = 静岡県神社庁 |url = http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4413057 |title = 岐佐神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 平成22年の修復事業により本殿の立て起こし、拝殿・覆屋の屋根瓦葺き替えなどを実施した。 === 摂末社 === 摂末社・境内関係社は次の通り。 '''境内社''' * ごしんさま（護神様・御親様）　：　天白社と、 伊勢神宮・津島神社・春日大社の分霊を祀る。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:きさしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:浜松市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:浜松市中央区の建築物]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]]

旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 旧暦9月14日・15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=岐佐神社|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''岐佐神社'''（きさじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|浜松市|Q185125}}{{jalink|中央区 (浜松市)|Q117084743|中央区}}舞阪町に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|Q11296639|蚶貝比賣命}} - アカガイの女神 * {{jalink|キサガイヒメ・ウムギヒメ|Q11296639|蛤貝比賣命}} - ハマグリの女神 === 概要 === [[File:Kis-jinja.jpg|thumb|
岐佐神社の鳥居
]] 『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』（927年）に、「{{jalink|遠江国|Q1048864}}六十二座、敷智郡六座の一座」との記載がある{{jalink|式内社}}。地域の氏神であると共に、水産・漁業の守り神として漁民の信仰を集めてきた{{Cite web|和書 |author = 岐佐神社 |url = https://www.kisa-jinja.jp/about |title = 岐佐神社の由緒 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|明応|Q1193841}}の津波（1498年）により、舞澤の郷と共に流されたが、東の松林（現在地）に神祠が漂着したため、その地に社殿を造営して祀った。 {{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}2年（1574年）に本殿を再建。{{jalink|慶長|Q1328185}}6年（1601年）には伊奈備前守より墨印石高三石を与えられ、三代将軍{{jalink|徳川家光|Q314481}}以下、歴代将軍より朱印五石を与えられた。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年、{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列せられた。{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}元年に本殿を造営、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}13年に神饌幣帛料供進社の指定を受けた{{Cite web|和書 |author = 静岡県神社庁 |url = http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4413057 |title = 岐佐神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 平成22年の修復事業により本殿の立て起こし、拝殿・覆屋の屋根瓦葺き替えなどを実施した。 === 摂末社 === 摂末社・境内関係社は次の通り。 '''境内社''' * ごしんさま（護神様・御親様）　：　天白社と、 伊勢神宮・津島神社・春日大社の分霊を祀る。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}}

Shrine |Image= [[File:Kisa-jinja-haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Hamamatsu City|Q185125}}{{jalink|Chūō-ku (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|Chūō Ward}}{{jalink|Maisaka Town|Q11613666}} Maisaka 1973 |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2 DMS|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{jalink|Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|Akagai-hime no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|Hamagai-hime no Mikoto}} |Rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Founded = Unknown |Honden = |Other name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = Lunar calendar September 14–15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Kisa Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Kisa Shrine''' (きさじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Maisaka Town, Chūō Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. === Enshrined Deities === * {{jalink|Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|Akagai-hime no Mikoto}} – Goddess of ark clams * {{jalink|Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|Hamagai-hime no Mikoto}} – Goddess of clams === Overview === [[File:Kis-jinja.jpg|thumb|
Torii gate of Kisa Shrine
]] In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'' (927), it is listed as “one of the six shrines of Shikichi District among the sixty-two shrines of {{jalink|Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864}},” making it a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. In addition to serving as the local tutelary shrine, it has long been revered by fishermen as a guardian deity of fisheries and fishing{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kisa Shrine |url = https://www.kisa-jinja.jp/about |title = History of Kisa Shrine |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === History === The shrine was washed away along with the village of Maisawa by the tsunami of the {{jalink|Meiō|Q1193841}} era (1498). However, when the divine shrine washed ashore in the pine grove to the east (the present location), a shrine building was constructed there and the deity was enshrined. The main hall was rebuilt in {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 2 (1574). In {{jalink|Keichō|Q1328185}} 6 (1601), three koku of land bearing an ink seal were granted by Ina Bizen-no-kami, and thereafter five koku bearing vermilion seals were granted by the third shogun {{jalink|Tokugawa Iemitsu|Q314481}} and successive shoguns. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6, the shrine was ranked as a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. In {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 1, the main hall was constructed, and in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 13 it was designated as a shrine eligible to receive offerings of sacred food and ritual expenses{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |url = http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4413057 |title = Kisa Shrine |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. A restoration project in Heisei 22 carried out the raising and realignment of the main hall, as well as re-roofing the worship hall and protective structures with tiles. === Subsidiary Shrines === The subsidiary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Precinct Shrines''' * Goshinsama (Guardian Deity / Ancestral Deity): Enshrines Tenpaku Shrine and divided spirits from Ise Grand Shrine, Tsushima Shrine, and Kasuga Taisha. === Notes === {{Footnote help}}

Shrine |Image= [[File:Kisa-jinja-haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Hamamatsu City|Q185125}}{{jalink|Chūō-ku (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|Chūō Ward}}{{jalink|Maisaka Town|Q11613666}} Maisaka 1973 |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2 DMS|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{jalink|Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|Akagai-hime no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|Hamagai-hime no Mikoto}} |Rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Founded = Unknown |Honden = |Other name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = Lunar calendar September 14–15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Kisa Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Kisa Shrine''' (きさじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Maisaka Town, Chūō Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. === Enshrined Deities === * {{jalink|Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|Akagai-hime no Mikoto}} – Goddess of ark clams * {{jalink|Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|Hamagai-hime no Mikoto}} – Goddess of clams === Overview === [[File:Kis-jinja.jpg|thumb|
Torii gate of Kisa Shrine
]] In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'' (927), it is listed as “one of the six shrines of Shikichi District among the sixty-two shrines of {{jalink|Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864}},” making it a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. In addition to serving as the local tutelary shrine, it has long been revered by fishermen as a guardian deity of fisheries and fishing{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kisa Shrine |url = https://www.kisa-jinja.jp/about |title = History of Kisa Shrine |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === History === The shrine was washed away along with the village of Maisawa by the tsunami of the {{jalink|Meiō|Q1193841}} era (1498). However, when the divine shrine washed ashore in the pine grove to the east (the present location), a shrine building was constructed there and the deity was enshrined. The main hall was rebuilt in {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 2 (1574). In {{jalink|Keichō|Q1328185}} 6 (1601), three koku of land bearing an ink seal were granted by Ina Bizen-no-kami, and thereafter five koku bearing vermilion seals were granted by the third shogun {{jalink|Tokugawa Iemitsu|Q314481}} and successive shoguns. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6, the shrine was ranked as a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. In {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 1, the main hall was constructed, and in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 13 it was designated as a shrine eligible to receive offerings of sacred food and ritual expenses{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |url = http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4413057 |title = Kisa Shrine |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. A restoration project in Heisei 22 carried out the raising and realignment of the main hall, as well as re-roofing the worship hall and protective structures with tiles. === Subsidiary Shrines === The subsidiary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Precinct Shrines''' * Goshinsama (Guardian Deity / Ancestral Deity): Enshrines Tenpaku Shrine and divided spirits from Ise Grand Shrine, Tsushima Shrine, and Kasuga Taisha. === Notes === {{Footnote help}}

Shrine |Image= [[File:Kisa-jinja-haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Hamamatsu City|Q185125}}{{jalink|Chūō-ku (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|Chūō Ward}}{{jalink|Maisaka Town|Q11613666}} Maisaka 1973 |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2 DMS|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{jalink|Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|Akagai-hime no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|Hamagai-hime no Mikoto}} |Rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Founded = Unknown |Honden = |Other name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = Lunar calendar September 14–15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Kisa Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Kisa Shrine''' (きさじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Maisaka Town, Chūō Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. === Enshrined Deities === * {{jalink|Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|Akagai-hime no Mikoto}} – Goddess of ark clams * {{jalink|Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|Hamagai-hime no Mikoto}} – Goddess of clams === Overview === [[File:Kis-jinja.jpg|thumb|
Torii gate of Kisa Shrine
]] In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'' (927), it is listed as “one of the six shrines of Shikichi District among the sixty-two shrines of {{jalink|Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864}},” making it a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. In addition to serving as the local tutelary shrine, it has long been revered by fishermen as a guardian deity of fisheries and fishing{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kisa Shrine |url = https://www.kisa-jinja.jp/about |title = History of Kisa Shrine |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === History === The shrine was washed away along with the village of Maisawa by the tsunami of the {{jalink|Meiō|Q1193841}} era (1498). However, when the divine shrine washed ashore in the pine grove to the east (the present location), a shrine building was constructed there and the deity was enshrined. The main hall was rebuilt in {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 2 (1574). In {{jalink|Keichō|Q1328185}} 6 (1601), three koku of land bearing an ink seal were granted by Ina Bizen-no-kami, and thereafter five koku bearing vermilion seals were granted by the third shogun {{jalink|Tokugawa Iemitsu|Q314481}} and successive shoguns. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6, the shrine was ranked as a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. In {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 1, the main hall was constructed, and in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 13 it was designated as a shrine eligible to receive offerings of sacred food and ritual expenses{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |url = http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4413057 |title = Kisa Shrine |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. A restoration project in Heisei 22 carried out the raising and realignment of the main hall, as well as re-roofing the worship hall and protective structures with tiles. === Subsidiary Shrines === The subsidiary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Precinct Shrines''' * Goshinsama (Guardian Deity / Ancestral Deity): Enshrines Tenpaku Shrine and divided spirits from Ise Grand Shrine, Tsushima Shrine, and Kasuga Taisha. === Notes === {{Footnote help}}

]] In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' (927), it is listed as “one of the six shrines of Shikichi District among the sixty-two shrines of {{ill|lt=Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864|WD=Q1048864}},” making it a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}. In addition to serving as the local tutelary shrine, it has long been revered by fishermen as a guardian deity of fisheries and fishing{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kisa Shrine |url = https://www.kisa-jinja.jp/about |title = History of Kisa Shrine |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === History === The shrine was washed away along with the village of Maisawa by the tsunami of the {{ill|lt=Meiō|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841}} era (1498). However, when the divine shrine washed ashore in the pine grove to the east (the present location), a shrine building was constructed there and the deity was enshrined. The main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 2 (1574). In {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 6 (1601), three koku of land bearing an ink seal were granted by Ina Bizen-no-kami, and thereafter five koku bearing vermilion seals were granted by the third shogun {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu|Q314481|WD=Q314481}} and successive shoguns. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. In {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 1, the main hall was constructed, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 13 it was designated as a shrine eligible to receive offerings of sacred food and ritual expenses{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |url = http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4413057 |title = Kisa Shrine |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. A restoration project in Heisei 22 carried out the raising and realignment of the main hall, as well as re-roofing the worship hall and protective structures with tiles. === Subsidiary Shrines === The subsidiary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Precinct Shrines''' * Goshinsama (Guardian Deity / Ancestral Deity): Enshrines Tenpaku Shrine and divided spirits from Ise Grand Shrine, Tsushima Shrine, and Kasuga Taisha. === Notes === {{Footnote help}}

Shrine |Image= [[File:Kisa-jinja-haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu City|Q185125|WD=Q185125}}{{ill|lt=Chūō-ku (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|WD=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward}}{{ill|lt=Maisaka Town|Q11613666|WD=Q11613666}} Maisaka 1973 |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2 DMS|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|WD=Q11296639|lt=Akagai-hime no Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|WD=Q11296639|lt=Hamagai-hime no Mikoto}} |Rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Founded = Unknown |Honden = |Other name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = Lunar calendar September 14–15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Kisa Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Kisa Shrine''' (きさじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Maisaka Town, Chūō Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. === Enshrined Deities === * {{ill|lt=Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|WD=Q11296639|lt=Akagai-hime no Mikoto}} – Goddess of ark clams * {{ill|lt=Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|WD=Q11296639|lt=Hamagai-hime no Mikoto}} – Goddess of clams === Overview === [[File:Kis-jinja.jpg|thumb|
Torii gate of Kisa Shrine
]] In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' (927), it is listed as “one of the six shrines of Shikichi District among the sixty-two shrines of {{ill|lt=Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864|WD=Q1048864}},” making it a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. In addition to serving as the local tutelary shrine, it has long been revered by fishermen as a guardian deity of fisheries and fishing{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kisa Shrine |url = https://www.kisa-jinja.jp/about |title = History of Kisa Shrine |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === History === The shrine was washed away along with the village of Maisawa by the tsunami of the {{ill|lt=Meiō|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841}} era (1498). However, when the divine shrine washed ashore in the pine grove to the east (the present location), a shrine building was constructed there and the deity was enshrined. The main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 2 (1574). In {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 6 (1601), three koku of land bearing an ink seal were granted by Ina Bizen-no-kami, and thereafter five koku bearing vermilion seals were granted by the third shogun {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu|Q314481|WD=Q314481}} and successive shoguns. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. In {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 1, the main hall was constructed, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 13 it was designated as a shrine eligible to receive offerings of sacred food and ritual expenses{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |url = http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4413057 |title = Kisa Shrine |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. A restoration project in Heisei 22 carried out the raising and realignment of the main hall, as well as re-roofing the worship hall and protective structures with tiles. === Subsidiary Shrines === The subsidiary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Precinct Shrines''' * Goshinsama (Guardian Deity / Ancestral Deity): Enshrines Tenpaku Shrine and divided spirits from Ise Grand Shrine, Tsushima Shrine, and Kasuga Taisha. === Notes === {{Footnote help}}

'ja'} [[JA:岐佐神社]] {{wikidata link|Q28682729}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Kisa Shrine |Image= [[File:Kisa-jinja-haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu City|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Chūō-ku (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|WD=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Maisaka Town|Q11613666|WD=Q11613666|1=Maisaka, Shizuoka}} Maisaka 1973 |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2 DMS|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|WD=Q11296639|lt=Akagai-hime no Mikoto|1=Kisagaihime and Umugihime}}
{{ill|lt=Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|WD=Q11296639|lt=Hamagai-hime no Mikoto|1=Kisagaihime and Umugihime}} |Rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Honden = |Other name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = Lunar calendar September 14–15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Kisa Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Kisa Shrine''' (きさじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Maisaka Town, Chūō Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. === Enshrined Deities === * {{ill|lt=Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|WD=Q11296639|lt=Akagai-hime no Mikoto|1=Kisagaihime and Umugihime}} – Goddess of ark clams * {{ill|lt=Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|WD=Q11296639|lt=Hamagai-hime no Mikoto|1=Kisagaihime and Umugihime}} – Goddess of clams === Overview === [[File:Kis-jinja.jpg|thumb|
Torii gate of Kisa Shrine
]] In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' (927), it is listed as “one of the six shrines of Shikichi District among the sixty-two shrines of {{ill|lt=Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864|WD=Q1048864|1=Tōtōmi Province}},” making it a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In addition to serving as the local tutelary shrine, it has long been revered by fishermen as a guardian deity of fisheries and fishing{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kisa Shrine |url = https://www.kisa-jinja.jp/about |title = History of Kisa Shrine |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === History === The shrine was washed away along with the village of Maisawa by the tsunami of the {{ill|lt=Meiō|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} era (1498). However, when the divine shrine washed ashore in the pine grove to the east (the present location), a shrine building was constructed there and the deity was enshrined. The main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 2 (1574). In {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 6 (1601), three koku of land bearing an ink seal were granted by Ina Bizen-no-kami, and thereafter five koku bearing vermilion seals were granted by the third shogun {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu|Q314481|WD=Q314481|1=Tokugawa Iemitsu}} and successive shoguns. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 1, the main hall was constructed, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 13 it was designated as a shrine eligible to receive offerings of sacred food and ritual expenses{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |url = http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4413057 |title = Kisa Shrine |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. A restoration project in Heisei 22 carried out the raising and realignment of the main hall, as well as re-roofing the worship hall and protective structures with tiles. === Subsidiary Shrines === The subsidiary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Precinct Shrines''' * Goshinsama (Guardian Deity / Ancestral Deity): Enshrines Tenpaku Shrine and divided spirits from Ise Grand Shrine, Tsushima Shrine, and Kasuga Taisha. === Notes === {{Footnote help}}

Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu City|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Chūō-ku (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|WD=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Maisaka Town|Q11613666|WD=Q11613666|1=Maisaka, Shizuoka}} Maisaka 1973 |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2 DMS|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|WD=Q11296639|lt=Akagai-hime no Mikoto|1=Kisagaihime and Umugihime}}
{{ill|lt=Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|WD=Q11296639|lt=Hamagai-hime no Mikoto|1=Kisagaihime and Umugihime}} |Rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Honden = |Other name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = Lunar calendar September 14–15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Kisa Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Kisa Shrine''' (きさじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Maisaka Town, Chūō Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. === Enshrined Deities === * {{ill|lt=Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|WD=Q11296639|lt=Akagai-hime no Mikoto|1=Kisagaihime and Umugihime}} – Goddess of ark clams * {{ill|lt=Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|WD=Q11296639|lt=Hamagai-hime no Mikoto|1=Kisagaihime and Umugihime}} – Goddess of clams === Overview === [[File:Kis-jinja.jpg|thumb|
Torii gate of Kisa Shrine
]] In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' (927), it is listed as “one of the six shrines of Shikichi District among the sixty-two shrines of {{ill|lt=Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864|WD=Q1048864|1=Tōtōmi Province}},” making it a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In addition to serving as the local tutelary shrine, it has long been revered by fishermen as a guardian deity of fisheries and fishing{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kisa Shrine |url = https://www.kisa-jinja.jp/about |title = History of Kisa Shrine |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === History === The shrine was washed away along with the village of Maisawa by the tsunami of the {{ill|lt=Meiō|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} era (1498). However, when the divine shrine washed ashore in the pine grove to the east (the present location), a shrine building was constructed there and the deity was enshrined. The main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 2 (1574). In {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 6 (1601), three koku of land bearing an ink seal were granted by Ina Bizen-no-kami, and thereafter five koku bearing vermilion seals were granted by the third shogun {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu|Q314481|WD=Q314481|1=Tokugawa Iemitsu}} and successive shoguns. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 1, the main hall was constructed, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 13 it was designated as a shrine eligible to receive offerings of sacred food and ritual expenses{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |url = http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4413057 |title = Kisa Shrine |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. A restoration project in Heisei 22 carried out the raising and realignment of the main hall, as well as re-roofing the worship hall and protective structures with tiles. === Subsidiary Shrines === The subsidiary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Precinct Shrines''' * Goshinsama (Guardian Deity / Ancestral Deity): Enshrines Tenpaku Shrine and divided spirits from Ise Grand Shrine, Tsushima Shrine, and Kasuga Taisha. === Notes === {{Footnote help}}

'ja'} [[JA:岐佐神社]] {{wikidata link|Q28682729}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Kisa Shrine |Image= [[File:Kisa-jinja-haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu City|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Chūō-ku (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|WD=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Maisaka Town|Q11613666|WD=Q11613666|1=Maisaka, Shizuoka}} Maisaka 1973 |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2 DMS|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|WD=Q11296639|lt=Akagai-hime no Mikoto|1=Kisagaihime and Umugihime}}
{{ill|lt=Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|WD=Q11296639|lt=Hamagai-hime no Mikoto|1=Kisagaihime and Umugihime}} |Rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Honden = |Other name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = Lunar calendar September 14–15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Kisa Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Kisa Shrine''' (きさじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Maisaka Town, Chūō Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. === Enshrined Deities === * {{ill|lt=Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|WD=Q11296639|lt=Akagai-hime no Mikoto|1=Kisagaihime and Umugihime}} – Goddess of ark clams * {{ill|lt=Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|WD=Q11296639|lt=Hamagai-hime no Mikoto|1=Kisagaihime and Umugihime}} – Goddess of clams === Overview === [[File:Kis-jinja.jpg|thumb|
Torii gate of Kisa Shrine
]] In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' (927), it is listed as “one of the six shrines of Shikichi District among the sixty-two shrines of {{ill|lt=Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864|WD=Q1048864|1=Tōtōmi Province}},” making it a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In addition to serving as the local tutelary shrine, it has long been revered by fishermen as a guardian deity of fisheries and fishing{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kisa Shrine |url = https://www.kisa-jinja.jp/about |title = History of Kisa Shrine |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === History === The shrine was washed away along with the village of Maisawa by the tsunami of the {{ill|lt=Meiō|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} era (1498). However, when the divine shrine washed ashore in the pine grove to the east (the present location), a shrine building was constructed there and the deity was enshrined. The main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 2 (1574). In {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 6 (1601), three koku of land bearing an ink seal were granted by Ina Bizen-no-kami, and thereafter five koku bearing vermilion seals were granted by the third shogun {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu|Q314481|WD=Q314481|1=Tokugawa Iemitsu}} and successive shoguns. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 1, the main hall was constructed, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 13 it was designated as a shrine eligible to receive offerings of sacred food and ritual expenses{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |url = http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4413057 |title = Kisa Shrine |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. A restoration project in Heisei 22 carried out the raising and realignment of the main hall, as well as re-roofing the worship hall and protective structures with tiles. === Subsidiary Shrines === The subsidiary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Precinct Shrines''' * Goshinsama (Guardian Deity / Ancestral Deity): Enshrines Tenpaku Shrine and divided spirits from Ise Grand Shrine, Tsushima Shrine, and Kasuga Taisha. === Notes === {{Footnote help}}

'ja'} [[JA:岐佐神社]] {{wikidata link|Q28682729}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Kisa Shrine |Image= [[File:Kisa-jinja-haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu City|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Chūō-ku (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|WD=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Maisaka Town|Q11613666|WD=Q11613666|1=Maisaka, Shizuoka}} Maisaka 1973 |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2 DMS|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|WD=Q11296639|lt=Akagai-hime no Mikoto|1=Kisagaihime and Umugihime}}
{{ill|lt=Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|WD=Q11296639|lt=Hamagai-hime no Mikoto|1=Kisagaihime and Umugihime}} |Rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Honden = |Other name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = Lunar calendar September 14–15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Kisa Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Kisa Shrine''' (きさじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Maisaka Town, Chūō Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. === Enshrined Deities === * {{ill|lt=Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|WD=Q11296639|lt=Akagai-hime no Mikoto|1=Kisagaihime and Umugihime}} – Goddess of ark clams * {{ill|lt=Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|WD=Q11296639|lt=Hamagai-hime no Mikoto|1=Kisagaihime and Umugihime}} – Goddess of clams === Overview === [[File:Kis-jinja.jpg|thumb|
Torii gate of Kisa Shrine
]] In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' (927), it is listed as “one of the six shrines of Shikichi District among the sixty-two shrines of {{ill|lt=Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864|WD=Q1048864|1=Tōtōmi Province}},” making it a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In addition to serving as the local tutelary shrine, it has long been revered by fishermen as a guardian deity of fisheries and fishing{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kisa Shrine |url = https://www.kisa-jinja.jp/about |title = History of Kisa Shrine |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === History === The shrine was washed away along with the village of Maisawa by the tsunami of the {{ill|lt=Meiō|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} era (1498). However, when the divine shrine washed ashore in the pine grove to the east (the present location), a shrine building was constructed there and the deity was enshrined. The main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 2 (1574). In {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 6 (1601), three koku of land bearing an ink seal were granted by Ina Bizen-no-kami, and thereafter five koku bearing vermilion seals were granted by the third shogun {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu|Q314481|WD=Q314481|1=Tokugawa Iemitsu}} and successive shoguns. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 1, the main hall was constructed, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 13 it was designated as a shrine eligible to receive offerings of sacred food and ritual expenses{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |url = http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4413057 |title = Kisa Shrine |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. A restoration project in Heisei 22 carried out the raising and realignment of the main hall, as well as re-roofing the worship hall and protective structures with tiles. === Subsidiary Shrines === The subsidiary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Precinct Shrines''' * Goshinsama (Guardian Deity / Ancestral Deity): Enshrines Tenpaku Shrine and divided spirits from Ise Grand Shrine, Tsushima Shrine, and Kasuga Taisha. === Notes === {{Footnote help}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu City|Q185125|qid=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Chūō-ku (Hamamatsu City)|Q117084743|qid=Q117084743|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Maisaka Town|Q11613666|qid=Q11613666|1=Maisaka, Shizuoka}} Maisaka 1973 |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2 DMS|34|41|2|N|137|36|39.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|qid=Q11296639|lt=Akagai-hime no Mikoto|1=Kisagaihime and Umugihime}}
{{ill|lt=Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|qid=Q11296639|lt=Hamagai-hime no Mikoto|1=Kisagaihime and Umugihime}} |Rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Honden = |Other name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = Lunar calendar September 14–15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Kisa Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Kisa Shrine''' (きさじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Maisaka Town, Chūō Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. === Enshrined Deities === * {{ill|lt=Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|qid=Q11296639|lt=Akagai-hime no Mikoto|1=Kisagaihime and Umugihime}} – Goddess of ark clams * {{ill|lt=Kisagaihime・Umugihime|Q11296639|qid=Q11296639|lt=Hamagai-hime no Mikoto|1=Kisagaihime and Umugihime}} – Goddess of clams === Overview === [[File:Kis-jinja.jpg|thumb|
Torii gate of Kisa Shrine
]] In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' (927), it is listed as “one of the six shrines of Shikichi District among the sixty-two shrines of {{ill|lt=Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864|qid=Q1048864|1=Tōtōmi Province}},” making it a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. In addition to serving as the local tutelary shrine, it has long been revered by fishermen as a guardian deity of fisheries and fishing{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Kisa Shrine |url = https://www.kisa-jinja.jp/about |title = History of Kisa Shrine |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. === History === The shrine was washed away along with the village of Maisawa by the tsunami of the {{ill|lt=Meiō|Q1193841|qid=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} era (1498). However, when the divine shrine washed ashore in the pine grove to the east (the present location), a shrine building was constructed there and the deity was enshrined. The main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 2 (1574). In {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 6 (1601), three koku of land bearing an ink seal were granted by Ina Bizen-no-kami, and thereafter five koku bearing vermilion seals were granted by the third shogun {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu|Q314481|qid=Q314481|1=Tokugawa Iemitsu}} and successive shoguns. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 1, the main hall was constructed, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 13 it was designated as a shrine eligible to receive offerings of sacred food and ritual expenses{{Cite web|Japanese |author = Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |url = http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4413057 |title = Kisa Shrine |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}. A restoration project in Heisei 22 carried out the raising and realignment of the main hall, as well as re-roofing the worship hall and protective structures with tiles. === Subsidiary Shrines === The subsidiary and related shrines within the precincts are as follows. '''Precinct Shrines''' * Goshinsama (Guardian Deity / Ancestral Deity): Enshrines Tenpaku Shrine and divided spirits from Ise Grand Shrine, Tsushima Shrine, and Kasuga Taisha. === Notes === {{Footnote help}}
